notes: Blind single-company menu for YUM: all 61 events reviewed in order plus 8 fiscal.ai KPIs; 63 candidates kept.

34 events yielded no candidate: 22 item-only boilerplate 8-Ks (officer/bylaw/vote/debt-agreement items with no real text), 9 generic 10-Q MD&A/risk-factor repeats, and 3 events (10-K 2025-02-19, 8-K 2023-08-02 press release, transcript 2025-11-04) whose material content was already quoted from other events.
All 8 fiscal.ai KPI labels were rewritten to standard driver names (brand + same_store_sales / unit_count); raw labels kept only as evidence_quote.

Some evidence quotes are analyst Q&A questions rather than management remarks (wage_inflation, labor_availability, menu_pricing, low_income_consumer_spending, kfc_china_system_sales, china_license_fees, nlrb_joint_employer_ruling, turkey_store_closures, kfc_international_same_store_sales) - real source text but lower authority.

Dedup decisions within YUM: middle_east_conflict kept over generic geopolitical-conflict wording; commodity_inflation kept over food-costs wording; restaurant_level_margin covers company-owned restaurant margin wording; investment_income covers the Devyani mark-to-market remeasurement; byte_platform_adoption names the Byte by Yum! SaaS platform (brand word By avoided as stopword); general_administrative_expenses used instead of g_and_a to avoid the glue word.
Evidence quotes preserve source typos and smart quotes exactly (e.g. 'China to to recover', 'personalized -to-one advertising', curly apostrophes in 10-K text).
